schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: **Where to worry (engineering flags):** 1. **Near rotor rim / fast-moving surfaces**: induced \(\mathbf E\) fields increase \(\mathcal E^{(u)}\) and Poynting flux — monitor energy density and radial shear here. 2. **Along field lines near dipole poles**: strong magnetic tension produces negative pressure (tension) that can pull on supports. Principal pressure eigenvectors often align with field lines — check bolt orientation. 3. **At interfaces / bearings**: relative flux \(\Delta\mathbf S=\mathbf S^{(u)}-\mathbf S^{(n)}\) indicates electromagnetic momentum deposition where torque and heating occur. Map \(|\Delta\mathbf S|\) along bearing surfaces. 4. **High shear zones where off-diagonals of \(S_{ij}\) are large**: these are locations where the material experiences shear stress cycles — fatigue risk.
theory.technical_interpretation: Executive Summary (One Page): Δmathbf S is an algebraic definition, written Δmathbf S=mathbf S^(u)-mathbf S^(n). It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from S, u, n, defining Δmathbf S. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
